concurrency_policy: no concurrent model jobs; no model jobs while user benchmarks are active

field_notes.requires_nvidia: Requires NVIDIA/Linux/CUDA stack, not merely Apple Silicon local acceleration.
field_notes.uses_local_accelerator: Apple MLX/MPS/GPU-style local acceleration may be used later; do not run now.

name: onevision_vlmaxxing_sequential_schedule

steps.hypothesis: A deterministic motion/residual Top-K allocator with full-frame anchors and THW visible indices reproduces the algorithmic surface needed for local tests.
steps.question: Can the clean-room patch allocator match OneVision's public codec input contract?
steps.skip_rule: If these tests fail, skip every downstream OneVision integration.
steps.success_gate: Unit tests pass for anchors, budget overflow, stable tie-breaking, toy score localization, temporal coverage, spatial-bias diagnostics, and macroblock fusion projection invariants.

steps.compute_lane: CPU plus optional PyAV research dependency
steps.eta_m3: 3-6 hours with video extraction after assets are restored
steps.eta_m5: 1-3 hours with video extraction after assets are restored
steps.hypothesis: Motion/residual fusion will expose whether anchor budget, temporal coverage, center bias, and edge/OCR starvation are sane enough to justify model runs.
steps.question: Do codec scores allocate tokens sensibly on existing paper videos before any VLM inference?
steps.setup_effort: real-video artifact path implemented; preflight checks source assets before render
steps.skip_rule: If raw videos are absent, restore benchmark assets first. Do not promote synthetic or generated-overlay artifacts as OV-1 evidence.
steps.success_gate: Render token-allocation-over-time plus selected-patch overlays for the three established clips when raw source videos are present; scheduled evidence must fail closed when any source clip is missing.

steps.defer_until: after OV-1 visual sanity or explicit override
steps.hypothesis: The safest first runnable integration is a continuous-score adapter over existing H.264 metadata, not a new hard BlockStatistic label.
steps.question: Can OneVision-style fused scores be consumed by existing Track A planners?
steps.setup_effort: implemented; pending final CPU-only checks and optional cached-input smoke
steps.skip_rule: If the adapter cannot reproduce existing Phase 1.29 codec-score behavior on cached/small inputs, do not start OV-3 model inference.
steps.success_gate: CLI flags, artifact schema, lazy-runtime guards, and regression tests exist for motion-only, residual-only, fused weighted, and max-age staleness gating in run_phase1_29_planner_accuracy_probe.py.

commands: uv run python scripts/run_phase1_29_planner_accuracy_probe.py --codec-score-source fused --fusion-mode weighted <dev args>
uv run python scripts/run_benchmark_track_a.py <promoted onevision planner args>
steps.compute_lane: local MLX one model at a time
steps.hypothesis: Continuous motion+residual scoring with per-item calibration will beat pixel max_abs and motion-only/residual-only ablations on paired answer stability at matched fresh budgets.
steps.question: Does OneVision-style scoring improve semantic substitution before real work is skipped?
steps.skip_rule: If fused scoring underperforms pixel max_abs on the first dev tranche, skip holdout promotion, skip OV-4/OV-6 model runs, and report a diagnostic related-work result.
steps.success_gate: No increase in parse failures, <= 1% paired-choice drift on gated dev cells, and a strict Pareto improvement in fresh-budget versus current Track A baselines.

commands: external: run upstream stage1/stage2 in an isolated NVIDIA/Linux environment
external: compare upstream visidx_thw.npy and positions_thw.npy against clean-room visible indices
steps.eta_m5: not recommended; CUDA/cv_reader stack is the wrong target
steps.hypothesis: After matching frame sampling, padding, grid size, anchors, and budget, allocator selection should match upstream under identical score planes; independent residual-score correlation is expected to be weaker unless we use cv_reader or an equivalent codec-internal residual extractor.
steps.question: Does the clean-room allocator agree with upstream OneVision preprocessing?
steps.setup_effort: NVIDIA/Linux environment plus cv_reader patched-FFmpeg build; add 2-4 hours first-time dependency setup before the parity run
steps.skip_rule: Defer this until OV-3 dev passes. If it fails, debug sampling, padding, score normalization, cv_reader residuals, and I-frame policy one variable at a time.
steps.success_gate: Identical-score Jaccard >= 0.90 for selected positions; independent-extraction results reported with separate motion-only and residual-only correlations rather than a single brittle Spearman gate.

steps.compute_lane: local MLX one model at a time
steps.hypothesis: If OneVision-style scoring is real rather than calibration noise, it should survive duration buckets, benchmark splits, and at least one cross-family check.
steps.question: Does a dev-positive fused codec planner transfer to holdout and cross-family slices?
steps.skip_rule: If holdout regresses, do not promote as a paper result; preserve as a bounded diagnostic.
steps.success_gate: Holdout cells remain parse-clean, preserve paired correctness within gate, and improve or match the current Track A Pareto frontier.

steps.compute_lane: M5 128GB preferred; no concurrent model jobs
steps.defer_until: after OV-5 or explicit high-risk override
steps.eta_m5: 16-28 hours Qwen, +12 hours Gemma only if Qwen gates
steps.hypothesis: Within already-fresh VLMaxxing regions, OneVision-style Top-K patch allocation may recover more task fidelity per token than current magnitude_norm keep-rate policies, but frozen towers are likely brittle.
steps.question: Can OneVision-style allocation improve real sparse vision work skipped?
steps.setup_effort: requires OV-3/OV-5 evidence and separate sparse-tower scorer wiring
steps.skip_rule: If the first Qwen dev tranche fails fidelity at all keep-rates, skip Gemma and treat the result as evidence that trained sparse encoders are needed.
steps.success_gate: At matched keep-rate, improve paired correctness/format over Phase 1.63J Qwen and Phase 1.63G Gemma boundaries while preserving measured vision-stage timing gains.

commands: manual future: transformers eager-attention smoke without flash_attention_2
manual future: MLX weight-conversion parity smoke if PyTorch/MPS is too slow
steps.compute_lane: local encoder-only first; NVIDIA only for official stack
steps.eta_m3: defer until machine free; smoke only
steps.eta_m5: defer until machine free; smoke plus small probes
steps.hypothesis: OV-Encoder can be a third vision-encoder probe locally, but it is not a third VLM comparable to Qwen/Gemma until an LMM head or probe stack is reproduced.
steps.question: Should OV-Encoder itself become a third local model family?
steps.setup_effort: 1-3 days for PyTorch/MPS feasibility with load plus single-clip forward parity; about 1 calendar week for a careful MLX port with weight conversion, 3D RoPE parity, and single-clip forward parity
steps.skip_rule: Skip if it requires flash-attention/CUDA for inference or if eager attention at target token counts exceeds local memory; move to NVIDIA only if encoder probes answer a paper-relevant question.
steps.success_gate: When the machine is free, run PyTorch/MPS or MLX-parity smoke on image and sparse-video encoder outputs; treat output-shape/parity as feasibility evidence, not QA accuracy.

steps.defer_until: after OV-6 or as accounting-only after OV-5
steps.eta_m3: 6-10 hours accounting only; model run deferred
steps.hypothesis: A OneVision-like cold ingest can reduce first-query vision work, while C-PERSIST dominates same-video follow-up latency; session-inclusive speedups must be reported by query count, not multiplied.
steps.question: How do cold-ingest sparse allocation and follow-up reuse compose without double counting?
steps.setup_effort: requires OV-6 pass for model-backed runtime; otherwise accounting-only
steps.skip_rule: If OV-6 has no fidelity-clean sparse-vision cell, use synthetic stage-share ceilings only and do not claim a working combined runtime.
steps.success_gate: Report setup-inclusive session curves for 1, 2, 5, 10, and 50 same-video questions with paired drift and stage-share accounting.

steps.defer_until: after OV-0 for plan table; after OV-3/OV-8 for result-filled table
steps.hypothesis: A fixed comparison table with status labels and denominator columns will prevent imported patch compression, Track A answer stability, Track B timing, and C-PERSIST session savings from being collapsed into one speedup number.
steps.question: How should OneVision, VLMaxxing, and combined techniques be compared without denominator drift?
steps.setup_effort: implemented planning artifact; update after experiments complete
steps.skip_rule: If model results are absent, emit the preregistered plan table with hypothesis rows only.
steps.success_gate: Emit JSON/CSV/Markdown rows for OneVision imported results, local patchification reproduction, VLMaxxing baselines, fused Track A, sparse Track B, C-PERSIST, combined session accounting, and OV-Encoder feasibility, including headline_numeric and target_to_beat fields.

commands: manual: draft editor feedback from completed artifacts
steps.defer_until: after OV-3 through OV-9 outcomes are known
steps.hypothesis: The editor-facing update will either add OneVision as trained codec-aligned related work plus diagnostic evidence, or promote a new combined runtime result only if Track B gates pass.
steps.question: What manuscript changes are justified after the evidence is in?
steps.skip_rule: Do not edit manuscript prose until this memo is reviewed.
steps.success_gate: Prepare an editor memo listing imported OneVision claims, reproduced local claims, failed hypotheses, decision-log reopen status, and exact paper sections that should change.
